keep_features_dicts_synced is a module-level decorator I'm adding (wraps func, re-syncs self._column_requires_decoding after call). _recursive_merge_dicts is a new module-level helper placed just above class Features, right before the require_decoding function block ends. update() now handles both plain overwrite and recursive dict-merge, and stays synced via the decorator. Does this cover your deep-merge requirement? Please send AGREE with identical code or a counter-DRAFT.

wraps needs 'from functools import wraps' import at top of features.py. keep_features_dicts_synced, _recursive_merge_dicts, __setitem__, __delitem__ are constructs only I touch (not overlapping per your confirmation). The ONLY construct we both write verbatim-identical is the update() method body shown above (decorated with keep_features_dicts_synced, containing the deep-merge loop). Please AGREE with this exact update() text, or propose changes.
